title: Cyathella cathayensis
intro: Cyathella cathayensis, also known as the Chinese Cyathella, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Cyathellaceae and is native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce stress, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cyathella cathayensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cyathella cathayensis is a species of lichen-forming fungus in the family Gomphillaceae. It belongs to the class Dothideomycetes and the phylum Ascomycota. The genus Cyathella is part of the order Gomphillales. Cyathella cathayensis is also known as a crustose lichen due to its growth habit. It is typically found in subtropical regions.
